# Implementation Plan: TW-0003
<!-- Generated by /plan -->

**Title:** Close remaining v0.2 deferred items (caret reveal, CM6 bench, a11y, reparse)
**Spec ID:** TW-0003
**Spec:** docs/specs/spec-TW-0003.md
**Plan size:** Standard

## Task

Close the four remaining TW-0002 deferred/partial items on the `ai/tw-0002` branch: (1) an **opt-in** caret-level per-marker reveal for unified mode built on a managed `contentEditable` (block-level stays the default, un-regressed); (2) a real CodeMirror-6 benchmark baseline published in `docs/BENCHMARKS.md`; (3) an `axe-core` accessibility sweep across the surfaces with fixes; (4) tightening `parseIncremental` to reparse only the dirty block(s) rather than to end-of-document. Correctness and no-regression over cleverness — the caret surface is additive and gated; the reparse change only narrows what is reparsed and stays deep-equal to full parse.

## Approach

All four land on `ai/tw-0002` (reuse `.worktrees/TW-0002`; docs stay in the main tree). Item 1 adds a new React surface (`CaretRevealBlock`) selected by a new `unifiedReveal` config, consuming the already-tested `hiddenMarkers(doc, sel)` (`core/unified.ts:120`) — the headless logic exists; this wires it to a live editable DOM. Item 4 edits `parseIncremental` internals (`core/parser.ts:1354` `runIncremental`) behind its existing property-test oracle. Items 2–3 are dev-tooling: add bench/test-only devDeps, run, publish. Nothing here is a new runtime dependency or a new package entry.

## Reference implementation

- Caret reveal: `demo/design-prototype.html` (unified per-line reveal idiom) is the visual truth. Code analogue: the existing `UnifiedEditor` block render + `SourceArea` in `src/react/TypewrightEditor.tsx` (the reveal replaces the whole-block textarea swap for a per-marker span toggle); `hiddenMarkers`/`collectMarkers` (`core/unified.ts:28,120`) provide the ranges; `TextDoc` / the `{from,to,insert}` DocChange shape (`core/text.ts`) is the commit contract.
- Reparse: the existing `runIncremental` reuse-prefix logic (`core/parser.ts:1354-1400`) and its oracle `parser.incremental.test.ts` (deep-equal to full parse over 1554 cases) — extend, don't replace.
- Bench: `bench/cm6-baseline.bench.ts` (the inert stub already present) + `bench/cold-parse.bench.ts` methodology; `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` publishing format.

## Prerequisites

- Bench/test-only devDependencies (never runtime deps / peers): `codemirror`, `@codemirror/lang-markdown` (+ `@codemirror/state`, `@codemirror/view` if not transitively present) for item 2; `@axe-core/playwright` (or `axe-core` + a wired call) for item 3. Install with `pnpm add -D` into the worktree. Confirm they land only in `devDependencies` and that `package.json` `dependencies` stays empty and no new `peerDependencies` appear.

## Steps

### 1. Config surface for the reveal mode
- **File:** `src/types.ts`
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Add `unifiedReveal?: 'block' | 'caret'` to `EditorConfig` (default `'block'`), documented: caret = per-marker reveal around the caret (opt-in; contentEditable-backed; IME via the platform). Additive only (semver — K-4). Do not touch `EditorMode`.
- **Reference:** the existing optional `EditorConfig` fields (`types.ts:271+`).
- **Verify:** `pnpm typecheck`; no existing type changed.

### 2. `CaretRevealBlock` — the managed contentEditable surface
- **File:** `src/react/CaretRevealBlock.tsx` (new) + `CARET_REVEAL_CSS` export
- **Action:** Create
- **Details:** Renders one block (or the doc's visible blocks) as `contentEditable`, showing rendered inline formatting with the raw markers wrapped in `<span class="tw-syntax" data-mark>` that are **hidden by default** and **revealed** (shown monospace) when the DOM selection is within/adjacent to that marker's source range, computed via `hiddenMarkers(doc, sel)` mapped to the block. On `beforeinput`/`input`, translate the DOM edit to a source `{from,to,insert}` splice against the block's range and emit `onChange` (string stays canonical). Map the DOM selection → source offset (walk text nodes accumulating source length, accounting for hidden marker spans which DO carry source width) to know the caret's source position each selection change. **IME:** listen for `compositionstart`/`compositionend` — suppress model sync during composition, commit the composed text to the model on `compositionend` (never reimplement composition). **Click-settle debounce (~40ms, SPEC §5.2 step 4):** defer the render→raw marker transition after a click so the DOM shift doesn't land mid-click and misplace the caret. Never `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` untrusted content — render text via React/DOM text nodes; markers are the only toggled spans (sanitizer boundary held). Accessible: the region is a labelled `textbox` (`role`, `aria-multiline`), content in the a11y tree.
- **Reference:** `hiddenMarkers`/`collectMarkers` (`core/unified.ts`); the block-commit splice in `UnifiedEditor` (`TypewrightEditor.tsx` ~302-315); SPEC §5.2 algorithm.
- **Verify:** unit-test the pure pieces (DOM-offset↔source-offset mapping; which markers reveal for a given selection) in vitest; behaviour in Phase-6 e2e.
- **Fulfils:** C-11 caret-level reveal.

### 3. Wire the reveal mode into the editor (gated)
- **File:** `src/react/TypewrightEditor.tsx`, `src/react/index.ts`
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** In unified mode, when `unifiedReveal === 'caret'` render blocks via `CaretRevealBlock` instead of the static-render + textarea-swap path; when `'block'` (default) keep today's exact behaviour. Inject `CARET_REVEAL_CSS` into the single stylesheet (concatenate, as the other component CSS is). Route the caret surface's commits through the same `commitValue`/`DocChange` path (so comment anchors, folds, incremental parse all keep working). Export `CaretRevealBlock` if useful. **Default path must be byte-identical** — the existing 39 e2e + unit tests stay green.
- **Reference:** the mode branch + `useInjectStyles` concatenation already in `TypewrightEditor.tsx`.
- **Verify:** existing suite green with default; e2e for the opt-in path (Phase 6).
- **Fulfils:** C-11 wiring; K (no regression).

### 4. `collectMarkers` coverage for fence/blockquote (reveal completeness)
- **File:** `src/core/unified.ts` (+ `unified.test.ts`)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Extend `collectMarkers` to also emit marker ranges for fenced-code ` ``` ` open/close lines and blockquote `>` prefixes (today it covers headings, emphasis/strong/strike, code, link/image, list, math, footnoteRef — fence/blockquote were deferred). This makes caret reveal complete for those constructs. Keep it pure and offset-exact.
- **Reference:** existing `collectMarkers` cases (`unified.ts:28-114`).
- **Verify:** unit tests for the new marker kinds; existing `unified.test.ts` green.
- **Fulfils:** C-11 completeness.

### 5. Tighten `parseIncremental` to dirty-block reparse
- **File:** `src/core/parser.ts` (+ `parser.incremental.test.ts`)
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** In `runIncremental` (`parser.ts:1354`), after choosing the reuse cut `k`, bound the reparsed region to the **dirty block(s) + a safe look-around** and **re-offset** the unchanged suffix blocks by the length delta, instead of `parseBlocks(nextLines.slice(c))` to EOF. Determine the dirty end from the change span; expand to the next safe blank-line boundary after the last affected block that isn't inside an open construct (fence/list/mdxFlow) — else fall back to reparse-to-EOF (still correct). Walk the reused suffix nodes shifting every `from`/`to` by `delta = insert.length - (to - from)`. **The property-test oracle is the contract:** it must stay deep-equal to full `parse(nextSrc)` for opts on and off across the existing corpus; ADD cases stressing a small edit near the TOP of a large doc (the case this optimizes). If re-offsetting nested nodes proves error-prone, keep reparse-to-EOF for that shape and only tighten the provable cases — never trade correctness for speed.
- **Reference:** `runIncremental` (`parser.ts:1354-1400`); `IncrementalStats.reparsedFromLine` (`:1263`) — add a `reparsedToLine`/reused-suffix stat for the instrumentation assertion.
- **Verify:** `parser.incremental.test.ts` deep-equal stays green; a new instrumentation test asserts a top-of-doc edit reparses a bounded region (not the whole tail); re-run `bench/cold-parse.bench.ts` and record the improved @mid number.
- **Fulfils:** SPEC §10 large-doc keystroke; FEATURES roadmap item.

### 6. CM6 benchmark baseline
- **File:** `bench/cm6-baseline.bench.ts`, `package.json` (devDeps), `docs/BENCHMARKS.md`
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Replace the inert stub with a real CodeMirror-6 cold-parse/keystroke baseline (`@codemirror/lang-markdown` over the same `bench/fixtures/*`), guarded so it skips cleanly if the devDeps are absent (keeps `pnpm bench` green for contributors who didn't install them). Run it; publish the real Typewright-vs-CM6 comparison table in `docs/BENCHMARKS.md`, replacing the "to be run" note — honest per SPEC §10 (named workloads, named competitor version, method).
- **Reference:** `bench/cold-parse.bench.ts` methodology; the existing BENCHMARKS.md format.
- **Verify:** `pnpm bench` runs green with the devDeps; BENCHMARKS.md shows real numbers + CM6 version.
- **Fulfils:** C-14.

### 7. Accessibility sweep
- **File:** `e2e/a11y.spec.ts` (new), fixes across `src/react/*.tsx` as surfaced
- **Action:** Create + Modify
- **Details:** Add a Playwright spec running `axe-core` against each surface driven in the demo: editor (all four modes incl. the new caret-reveal), toolbar, comments sidebar, settings panel, ⌘K palette, fold menu, table grid. Fail on serious/critical violations in *our* markup. Fix the violations (missing roles/labels/`aria-*`, focus order, focus traps, name-from-content) surgically in the components. Contrast/theme issues that are the host's to set are documented, not force-fixed.
- **Reference:** existing e2e idioms (`e2e/*.spec.ts`); the components' current partial ARIA.
- **Verify:** the a11y spec passes (green twice with the rest); document any accepted/host-scope items.
- **Fulfils:** C-15.

### 8. Docs in step
- **File:** `docs/FEATURES.md`, `README.md`
- **Action:** Modify
- **Details:** Flip the caret-reveal row to ✅ (with a one-line note: opt-in via `unifiedReveal:'caret'`; IME handled via contentEditable rather than the SPEC's exact hidden-sink); update the IME/reveal roadmap line; note CM6 numbers + a11y pass. Keep the honesty caveat on the deep-IME tail. If nothing else remains 🟡, say the featureset is spec-complete.
- **Verify:** FEATURES.md accurate to shipped state; README claims match.
- **Fulfils:** C-16 / honesty.

## Edge cases

- **contentEditable + React**: React must not fight the DOM the browser edits — treat the editable region as uncontrolled during editing (write to the model on input/commit, re-render from model only when not composing/actively editing), or the caret jumps. This is the core correctness risk; test caret stability explicitly.
- **DOM-offset ↔ source-offset mapping** must account for hidden marker spans (zero visual width but real source width) and for rendered entities (`&amp;` = 1 char). Get this wrong and the caret maps to the wrong source position → wrong marker reveal / wrong edit target.
- **IME composition**: model sync during `compositionstart`→`compositionend` corrupts the composition; suppress it and commit once on end.
- **Reparse re-offset**: a nested node whose `from`/`to` isn't shifted desyncs decorations; the property test catches it — widen the fallback rather than ship a wrong tree.
- **Bench devDeps absent**: `pnpm bench` must still pass (skip CM6 cleanly), or contributors without the opt-in deps get a red gate.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With `unifiedReveal:'caret'`, caret inside `**bold**` reveals the `**`; moving the caret away hides them again — per marker, not per block; the default `'block'` mode is byte-identical to today (existing 39 e2e green).
- [ ] Clicking rendered text in caret mode places the caret at the correct source position without a visible jump; typing inserts at the right place and the document round-trips to canonical Markdown.
- [ ] Basic IME/compose input (driven via CDP in e2e) commits the composed text correctly to the model in caret mode.
- [ ] `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` publishes real Typewright-vs-CodeMirror-6 numbers on the named fixtures with the CM6 version; `pnpm bench` is green with and without the opt-in devDeps.
- [ ] `axe-core` reports no serious/critical violations in our markup across editor/toolbar/comments/settings/palette/fold-menu/table-grid (or each exception is documented as host-scope).
- [ ] `parseIncremental` reparses a bounded region for a top-of-doc edit (instrumented assertion) and stays deep-equal to full `parse` across the property corpus (opts on and off); the large-doc @mid keystroke number is re-measured and improved.
- [ ] `docs/FEATURES.md` has no remaining deferred rows for these items; all gates green (typecheck / unit / e2e twice / build / size).

## Verify

- Per item: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm vitest run --exclude '**/parser.perf.test.ts'`, then `pnpm e2e` (green twice), plus a manual pass in the demo toggling `unifiedReveal:'caret'` across light/dark. Security-relevant (caret surface): re-run the XSS e2e; confirm no compiled/untrusted innerHTML. Release close: `pnpm build`, `pnpm size`, `pnpm bench`.

## Out of scope

- A separate `src/core/view/` hidden-input-sink engine (SPEC §4.4 literal) — the user-facing goal (caret reveal + working IME) is met via contentEditable; documented as an architectural divergence, not a gap.
- Making caret-level the default (stays opt-in this release).
- Deep bidi/RTL hardening beyond contentEditable's native behaviour.
